text: A genome-wide association study analyzed 425 foxtail millet samples from Xinjiang Academy using 1,304,248 SNPs. 77 QTL regions were detected across three environments. Analysis of LD, genetic structure, clustering, and phylogeny showed regional differences among foxtail millet subgroups.
ABA Insensitive 5 (ABI5) is a basic leucine zipper transcription factor. There is minimal research on the ABI5 family in foxtail millet.

Molecular markers identified chromosomal regions with powdery mildew resistance genes and estimated each locus's resistance effect. Twenty-eight F-1 hybrids and eight parental lines from a barley diallel study were inoculated with five isolates of E. graminis.

Marker-assisted breeding is important for genetic improvement. This study aimed to identify QTL associated with bioenergy-related traits in sweet sorghum, including flowering time, plant height, total biomass, stem diameter, stem moisture percentage, and brix.

CRISPR components targeting the sorghum genome were delivered to protoplasts via polyethylene glycol-mediated transfection, both plasmid-mediated and ribonucleoprotein-based. Targeted deep sequencing analyzed frequencies of small insertions and deletions induced by sgRNAs targeting four endogenous sorghum genes.

Protoplasts were isolated from sorghum leaves. Plants were cultivated under three conditions. Cultivation with seven days of a 16-h light/8-h dark photoperiod followed by three days of continuous darkness yielded the highest viable protoplasts and transfection efficiency.
Clust analysis found 2987 genes correlated with increasing CC and RWC. Pathway analysis identified ribosome as a positive regulator of RWC and thermogenesis as a positive regulator of CC.
Over 240 QTLs are reported for drought tolerance traits in sorghum for marker discovery. Identifying traits and understanding their physiological and genetic mechanisms may enhance HT tolerance.
The waxy gene is crucial for starch synthesis in foxtail millet endosperm. Researchers developed a KASP marker, KASP-Waxy, from the waxy gene sequence to identify waxy foxtail millet.
The SiWD40-1 gene in foxtail millet is a key regulator of drought stress. Overexpression of SiWD40-1 in Arabidopsis thaliana improved drought tolerance.

Quinoa seedlings were treated with 24-epibrassinolide (EBR). CqBIN2 was transiently transferred to the quinoa seedlings' leaves using VIGS technology. EBR treatment increased root growth parameters, antioxidant enzyme activities, and osmolyte content. It decreased H2O2, O2 center dot-, and malondialdehyde content.

Four candidate genes for sdw3 were identified within barley BAC sequences. They co-segregated with sdw3 or were adjacent to co-segregating genes. This identification provides tools for marker-assisted selection.
12 QTL for leaf Delta C-13 were detected in the W x I RILs. 5 QTL were detected in the M x H RILs. For the W x I RILs, a major QTL was located on chromosome 3H near marker Bmag606.

This study identified 5 QTLs for thousand grain weight (TGW), 9 for grain length (GL), and 1 for grain width (GW) using data from 202 RIL populations. One co-located QTL for TGW was discovered on chromosome 1. Transcriptome analysis revealed 59 candidate genes with significant expression differences during grain development.

A recombinant inbred line population derived from a cross between DZZ and KL10 was developed. A high-density genetic map was constructed using genotyping-by-sequencing data, and QTL analysis was performed across five environments.
Several SiLRR-RLK genes are involved in stress response pathways. SiLRR-RLK genes show differential expression in response to drought, salinity, and pathogen infection.
RT-qPCR verified the transcriptome data. Inhibiting SiPME67 expression with antisense Oligonucleotide caused abnormal pollen tube subapical growth.

GWAS identified MTAs for oat nutritional quality, agronomic, and milling traits in spring oat germplasm. High-density sequence-based markers (15,037) were identified in 1,092 lines evaluated in 11 field conditions over three years.
FtbHLH2 localizes in the nucleus. Its overexpression in Arabidopsis increases cold tolerance.

Comparative genomics and QTL analysis revealed the genetic basis of the easily dehulled trait in a particular variety. This variety was artificially selected by the Wa people, who cultivate Tartary buckwheat as a staple food to prevent lysine deficiency.

Sorghum plants were exposed to abiotic stress treatments. Hsf expression was not observed with ABA treatment. Hsf1 showed high expression during high temperature and cold stresses. Hsf4 and Hsf6 showed expression during salt stress. Hsf5, Hsf6, Hsf10, Hsf13, Hsf19, Hsf23 and Hsf25 showed expression during drought stress.

We phenotyped 590 diverse sorghum accessions from West Africa in 10 environments under drought stress conditions: preflowering water stress (WS1), postflowering water stress (WS2), well-watered (WW), and rainfed (RF) over 4 years. Traits measured include days to 50% flowering (DFLo), aboveground dry biomass (DBM), plant height (PH), and grain yield components: grain weight (GrW), panicle weight (PW), grain number (GrN) per plant, and 1000-grain weight (TGrW). Genome-wide association studies (GWAS) were conducted. Broad-sense heritability for biomass and grain yield was 33-92% across environments.

Markers for chlorophyll content and leaf traits were linked to genes including PDCD4, LEA proteins, and Cytochrome P450. These findings provide molecular markers and genotypes for genomics-assisted breeding in sorghum.
Of 8,361 high-quality InDels longer than 20 bp developed as molecular markers, 180 were validated with 91.5% accuracy. SSR41 and these markers were used to map the white leaf sheath gene SiWLS1.
GsNAC2 overexpression upregulated key glutathione biosynthesis genes. It increased GR and GSH-Px activities and accumulated more GSH after saline-alkali treatment.
bZIPs are crucial for abiotic stress resistance. Setaria italica is a model for understanding stress resistance mechanisms.

Jingu 21 resistance to 31 herbicides was categorized into five levels: extremely weak, weak, moderate, strong, and extremely strong. Jingu 21 showed extremely strong resistance to lactofen, butachlor, and anilofos.

The study identified 11 QTLs for five traits under drought stress (DS) and well-watered (WW) conditions. qPLAL4.1, a major QTL for leaf rolling, was detected on chromosome 4. Two candidate genes, SiLTP and SiPLATZ12, were identified within the qPLAL4.1 region.

In foxtail millet, SiARDP gene expression was induced by various abiotic stresses, including ABA, PEG, and NaCl treatments. Overexpression of SiARDP in Arabidopsis enhanced tolerance to drought and salt stresses.
A major QTL for flowering time, qFt3.1, was identified on chromosome 3. Overexpression of the candidate gene SiCOL15 delayed flowering time in foxtail millet.

The genetic effect of lnt2 was verified in two other genetic backgrounds, explaining variances of 86.43% and 91.01% in tiller numbers between lines with lnt2 mutant and wild-type alleles. A large F2 population was constructed and lnt2 was fine-mapped.
PUB genes play a role in regulating salt stress in sorghum. PUB genes might serve as targets for breeding salt-tolerant sorghum.
Foxtail millet is a reserve crop for extreme weather and a model crop for C4 gene research. Analyzing its flowering regulatory network is important for cultivating early-maturing varieties.
Drought tolerance traits, stay green, and grain yield have been studied. Traits such as restricted transpiration and root architecture need to be explored and used in breeding.

This analysis included 337 QTL for abiotic stress tolerance traits. It identified 79 metaQTL (MQTL): 26 for drought, 11 for low temperature, 22 for salinity, 17 for water-logging, and 3 for mineral toxicity and deficiency. The MQTL distribution was similar to the initial QTL distribution.

833 DEGs were found in cultivar JS6 and 2166 DEGs in cultivar NM5 under simulated drought using 20% PEG-6000 for 6 hours (JS6T6, NM5T6) and 24 hours (JS6T24, NM5T24). The DEGs in JS6T6 and JS6T24 were 0.298-fold and 0.754-fold higher than in NM5T6 and NM5T24, respectively. More DEGs were found in the T6 treatments than in the T24 treatments compared to their controls.

BY utilized sugars more effectively via sugar consumption. Amino acids strengthened metabolism and upregulated lignin, which were positive responses of BY roots to saline-alkali stress. This led to higher osmotic adjustment of solute concentrations and cell growth. YZY mainly used soluble sugars and flavonoids combined with sugars to form glycosides as osmotic regulatory or antioxidant substances to cope with saline-alkali stress.

RNA-sequencing analysis of foxtail millet pollen, ovule, glume, seedling, and root revealed 940 up-regulated genes in pollen. These up-regulated genes are involved in pollen tube growth processes including receptor kinases, small peptides, calcium signaling, small G proteins, vesicle transport, cytoskeleton, cell wall correlation, and transcription factors.

Restriction site polymorphisms were used to investigate co-location of candidate genes with QTL for seedling drought stress-induced premature senescence in cowpea. Genomic DNA from 113 F-2:8 RILs of drought-tolerant IT93K503-1 and susceptible CB46 genotypes was digested with EcoR1, HpaII, Mse1, or Msp1 restriction enzymes and amplified with primers from 13 drought-responsive cDNAs.

Early season cold affects seed germination, seedling vigor, and root architecture in sorghum. Late-season frost reduces fertility, yield, and nutrition quality and content in sorghum. An association mapping study was conducted to delineate the genetics of early season cold and late-season frost tolerance in sorghum.

The genetic structure of 142 sorghum parent lines was analyzed using a model-based approach with SSR markers. Forty-one SSR markers were selected from 103 markers. These markers generated 189 alleles, ranging from 2 to 11 per marker, with an average of 4.6.

The locus N1 controls the covered/hulless character. N1 affected most measured traits. Additive QTL models with N1 as a covariate were superior to models based on separate covered and hulless sub-populations.

Promoter analysis revealed abundant hormone- and stress-responsive cis-elements. Tissue-specific RNA-seq data showed root-enriched expression of SbDIR2, SbDIR4, SbDIR18, SbDIR39, SbDIR44, and SbDIR53, implicating their roles in root development.

SbCASP4 improves salt tolerance in sweet sorghum by enhancing the root apoplastic barrier and blocking sodium ion transport to the shoot. Sweet sorghum is a C4 crop tolerant to salt, drought, and waterlogging.
Hsfs4 and Hsfs22 are highly expressed in panicle. Hsfs4 and Hsfs9 are expressed in seedling.

Quinoa is an economic crop. Drought affects quinoa yield. Clarifying quinoa adaptation to drought is conducive to cultivating drought-tolerant varieties. The study of quinoa drought stress-related metabolism and identification of related metabolites are unknown.

443 genotype-specific high NH4+-responsive DEGs with sequence variation were identified by integrating transcriptome and genome re-sequencing data. TFs including MYB, MADS, and LBD genes and RLKs such as FtBAM1/3 may help adapt to high NH4+.

DEP in Bai5 is classified into protein processing in the endoplasmic reticulum, estrogen signaling pathway, antigen processing and presentation, longevity regulating pathway-multiple species, arginine and proline metabolism, beta-alanine metabolism, vitamin B6 metabolism, salmonella infection, chloroalkane and chloroalkene degradation, and limonene and pinene degradation. DEP in Vao-9 are classified as ribosome and carbon fixation pathways in prokaryotes, pentose and glucuronate interconversions, GABA ergic synapse, and taurine and hypotaurine metabolism.

F-1 and F-2 plants inoculated with C. graminicola isolates showed that resistance to anthracnose in sorghum accession G 73 segregated as a recessive trait in a cross with susceptible cultivar HC 136. A PCR-based SCAR marker was developed for marker-assisted selection in sorghum breeding.
Two minor QTLs (qBX3.1 and qBX7.1) for Brix (BX) were mapped on chromosomes 3 and 7. The QTLs in two clusters (qPH7.1/qBX7.1 and qPH7.1/qFBW7.1) overlapped for PH, FBW and BX.

A drought tolerance QTL on chromosome 2 was transferred from barley cultivar 'Tadmor' to cultivars 'Baronesse' and 'Aydanhanim'. The QTL's effects on drought tolerance and other traits were studied using near-isogenic lines in controlled environments and field trials.

The two AFLP markers were converted to STS markers. The STS markers are useful for marker-assisted selection of plants with non-brittle pedicel and for map-based cloning of the non-brittle pedicel gene.

Transcriptome data showed high expression of genes VfbZIP1, VfbZIP2, VfbZIP5, VfbZIP7, VfbZIP15, VfbZIP17, and VfbZIP18 in a drought-tolerant cultivar under drought stress. qRT-PCR confirmed consistent expression of VfbZIP1, VfbZIP2, and VfbZIP5. Ectopic overexpression of VfbZIP5 in tobacco using a PVX vector enhanced drought tolerance.

The transcriptome findings suggested that 2253 differentially expressed genes (DEGs) are drought-responsive genes influenced by SiNRX1. The DEGs were enriched in pathways including phenylalanine, tyrosine and tryptophan biosynthesis, plant hormone signal transduction, phenylpropanoid biosynthesis, and plant-pathogen interaction.

The Milletdb database contains GWAS results for 20 yield-related traits. These traits were measured under three environmental conditions: field (no stress), early drought, and late drought over two years.

Sacaca and QQ57 exhibited high salt tolerance thresholds, low salt sensitivity index, and high Na+ sequestration capacity into vacuoles. A positive association was detected between salt tolerance degree and yield parameters, saponins (SAPs), and minerals content.

Inhibiting the expression of the FtIST1 gene in Tartary buckwheat by VIGS decreased the activities of antioxidant enzymes SOD, POD, and CAT. It increased plasmalemma permeability and leaf Na+ content. This indicates that loss of FtIST1 gene expression leads to decreased antioxidant capacity, increased cell membrane damage, increased shoot Na+ content, and decreased salt tolerance. FtIST1 is a salt tolerance gene associated with salt tolerance in Tartary buckwheat.

The drought-responsive gene ZmVQ52 from maize was cloned and characterized. Overexpression of ZmVQ52 in Arabidopsis enhanced drought tolerance by increasing proline content and decreasing malondialdehyde (MDA) content.
The results showed that the expression of the SiMYB1 gene was induced by drought, salt, and ABA. Overexpression of SiMYB1 in transgenic Arabidopsis improved drought and salt tolerance.
A major QTL for kernel number per spike (KNPS) in wheat was identified on chromosome 4B, designated as QKnps.caas-4B. It was consistently expressed in multiple environments.

Parent lines should be selected for higher heterosis based on different agronomical traits and distant genetic distance. Hybrid breeding should consider groups clustered by agronomical traits and molecular markers.

Sorghum overcomes limitations of model species like Arabidopsis thaliana. Advances in metabolomics, transcriptomics, proteomics, phenomics, population genomics and pangenomics expand understanding of Sorghum's drought resilience mechanisms.
An F-2 population was generated from a cross between sweet sorghum cv. 'Erdurmus' and grain sorghum cv. 'Ogretmenoglu'. A genetic map was constructed from SNPs discovered by ddRAD-seq.
Sorghum callus has metabolites. The epigenetic mechanism regulating metabolic biosynthesis in sorghum is unknown.
The marker OPA 12(383) was 6.03 cM from the locus for anthracnose resistance. The marker OPA 12(383) was cloned and sequenced.
Flowering time was recorded to confirm identification of known QTL. The study combined field visual ratings and reflectance data to identify GWAS marker-trait associations using GBS data.

Drought and HT tolerance can be improved by understanding tolerance mechanisms, screening germplasm to identify tolerant lines, and incorporating those traits into elite breeding lines. Systems approaches identify the best tolerance donors for SSA and SA sorghum breeding programs.

Overexpression of SiCDPK24 in Arabidopsis enhanced drought resistance and improved survival under drought stress. It activated the expressions of stress-related genes RD29A, RD29B, RD22, KIN1, COR15, COR47, LEA14, CBF3/DREB1A, and DREB2A.

The genetic variation and basis of A, E and A:E were described among 70 recombinant inbred lines (RILs) of sorghum. Experiment 1 used 40% and 80% of field capacity as water regimes. Experiment 2 used 80% field capacity.

The sweet sorghum SbWRKY50 gene (Sb09g005700) encodes a typical class II WRKY family protein. SbWRKY50 localizes to the nucleus and has transcriptional activation activity. Its expression in sweet sorghum was reduced by salt stress. Ectopic expression of SbWRKY50 reduced salt tolerance in Arabidopsis thaliana plants.

For sorghum SSR markers, 2,113 primer pairs were designed from 81,342 public genomic contigs. Screening eight sorghum lines identified 1,758 polymorphic primers. Of these, 1,710 SSR markers were predominantly polymorphic and detected from two to eight alleles.
369 SNPs were associated with six grain traits. 30 SNPs were detected in multiple years.

Twenty-five sorghum accessions were evaluated. The LW7 marker identified accessions with the male sterility gene rf4 in Suphan Buri1 and 03B cultivars. These genotypes lacked the 779 bp band. The cross between Suphan Buri1 and the male-sterile line 03A produced a sterile male, confirming the marker's usefulness in breeding.

entities.text: cross between Suphan Buri1 and the male-sterile line 03A
entities.label: CROSS

text: Two sorghum varieties, drought-sensitive Jinza 35 (V1) and drought-tolerant Longza 24 (V2), were studied under drought conditions. Transcriptomic analysis revealed distinct molecular mechanisms between the varieties. Both varieties showed drought-responsive changes in photosynthesis-related pathways.

The identified QTL include those in regions linked with seedling resistance genes Pc48, Pc58a, Pc68, Pc71, Pc91, and PcKM, and with adult-plant resistance and adaptation-related QTL. QTL on linkage groups Mrg03, Mrg08, and Mrg23 were identified in regions not previously associated with crown rust resistance.

The drought-tolerant variety V2 showed enrichment in phenylpropanoid biosynthesis, starch-sucrose metabolism, and plant hormone signaling pathways. Variety V1 activated ribosome metabolism and cell cycle regulation pathways.

Hexaploid amphiploids have the AACCDD genome composition, while octoploid amphiploids have AACCCCDD, AAAACCDD, or AABBCCDD compositions. The A, B, C, and D oat genomes differ in involvement in non-centromeric, intercalary translocations.

Sweet sorghum accumulates sugars in the stem parenchyma. Soluble acid invertase (SAI) is involved in sucrose accumulation. Characterization of SAI genes and development of functional markers are important for marker-assisted selection.

Drought stress increased malondialdehyde (MDA) and hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) levels, with the highest accumulation in L3. Cultivar D maintained higher antioxidant enzyme activities (CAT, POD, SOD, APX), better photosynthetic performance, and greater chlorophyll retention than L3.

The study identified two zinc finger knuckle genes, Ca04468 and Ca07571, as candidates for drought response in chickpea. Sanger sequencing, DArT, molecular markers, RT-qPCR, and field trial seed trait associations were used for their analysis.

In the cultivar BY5, most metabolite concentrations increased after exposure to 150 mM NaCl but decreased after exposure to 300 mM NaCl. Transcriptomic analysis revealed that gene expressions in BY5 were significantly affected under 300 mM NaCl exposure (34040 genes up-regulated and 14757 genes down-regulated).

Salt-tolerant and salt-sensitive quinoa cultivars showed induced expression levels of CqGSTs genes in roots under salt treatment. Most quinoa bHLH transcription factors have high homology with Arabidopsis thaliana bHLH family members.

Dehydration response element binding proteins (DREBs) are transcriptional regulators that enhance plant tolerance to abiotic stress. This study analyzed the DREB gene family (SiDREBs) in foxtail millet (Setaria italica) using bioinformatics and gene expression under abiotic stress.

APR provides broad-spectrum resistance to Pca populations. The 11 oat genotypes with APR are desirable resistance sources for breeding programs developing durable crown rust resistant cultivars for eastern Canada.
Yellow endosperm sorghum contains carotenoids. The objective was to study the genetic basis of carotenoid variation by mapping QTL associated with carotenoid content and endosperm color.

Nineteen genes for anthocyanin biosynthesis were annotated in this genomic region. Gene Si1g06530 encodes a SANT/Myb domain protein and was highly expressed in developing panicles and seeds. It is a candidate gene for the brown hull color of Jigu 31.

The two materials show distinct accumulation patterns of secondary metabolites phenylpropanoids, including hydroxycinnamates and flavonoids. This may contribute to the improved drought resistance of the novel tartary buckwheat cultivars.

BC lines had moderate to high CLS and PM resistance compared to susceptible parent KING. BC line H3 had similar or superior agronomic traits to KING and higher yield (18.0-32.0%) under CLS and PM outbreaks.
The objective of this study was to identify quantitative trait loci (QTL) for grain yield and its components under drought stress in a recombinant inbred line (RIL) population of barley.
The results indicated that the expression of SiARDP was induced by cold stress, and overexpression of SiARDP in Arabidopsis improved the cold tolerance of transgenic plants.

QTLs for biological yield, days to heading, and kernel weight on chromosome 2H; QTL for harvest index on 1H; QTLs for kernel weight on 2H, 4H, and 5H. For rainfall less than 450 mm, QTL for grain yield on 6H; QTLs for kernel weight on 2H and 5H; QTLs for seed per head on 1H and 6H; QTL for days to heading on 2H.

A study identified the Alkaline tolerance 1 (AT1) gene in sorghum. AT1 is associated with alkaline tolerance and encodes a G protein gamma-subunit. Editing AT1 enhances yields of sorghum, rice, maize, and millet in alkaline soils.

A high quality chromosome-scale genome assembly of little millet comprises 18 chromosomes and 59,045 genes. Eleven chromosomes are assembled from telomere to telomere, revealing an 850 Mb tetraploid genome.

Six sterile and six restorer lines of sorghum and 36 hybrid sorghum combinations from incomplete double-row crosses were tested. The root bleeding sap of the F1 hybrid sorghum showed high heterosis for soluble sugar content and amino acid content at flowering stage, with average high-parent heterosis of 129.34% and 74.57%, respectively.
A linkage map used 149 markers (RAPD, AFLP, ISSR, SSR, and 2 morphological markers) on 11 linkage groups (LGs). Analysis of variance for FT was significant across locations and years.

Under saline-alkali stress, SiDREB153 expression differed between resistant and susceptible materials. Under drought, saline-alkali, and shade stress, SiDREB80 expression increased. Under saline-alkali and high temperature stress, SiDREB4, SiDREB129, and SiDREB131 expression increased. Under herbicide and saline-alkali stress, SiDREB159 expression increased.

A study of 115 sorghum accessions found that the average germination rate under salt stress was 55.4%, and the salt tolerance index was 0.65. The study identified 11 salt-tolerant accessions, including JP-2-1, with a salt tolerance index of 0.98.
The SbWRKY30 gene in sorghum was cloned and found to be located on chromosome 3. Overexpression of SbWRKY30 in transgenic Arabidopsis enhanced salt tolerance.
Flooding stress induces the expression of the C2H2 gene, which is related to waterlogging tolerance in sorghum.
The average plant height of foxtail millet is 150 cm, and the grain protein content is 12.5%.
